title: Ayapana hylophila
taxonomy: Ayapana hylophila is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family consists of flowering plants that are divided into several subfamilies, including the Mutisioideae subfamily. Within the Asteraceae family, Ayapana hylophila belongs to the tribe Eupatorieae. The genus Ayapana is a group of plants that are native to Central and South America, and are characterized by their aromatic leaves and small flowers. The species Ayapana hylophila is a specific type of plant within the Ayapana genus, known for its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Ayapana hylophila, also known as "santa maria," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ayapana hylophila include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Ayapana hylophila is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, respiratory issues, and skin problems, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
